mnemonics: ACID Rain Keeps Data Safe: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability - the four properties of a reliable transaction.
keyPoints: A transaction is a single logical unit of work that accesses and possibly modifies the contents of a database.
The ACID properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) guarantee database transactions are processed reliably.
Concurrency control protocols like locking and timestamp ordering manage simultaneous operations to prevent conflicts.
commonMistakes: Assuming a COMMIT is unnecessary if no data was changed, forgetting that a transaction's logical operations (like a read) still need to be formally ended.
Confusing system crash (which affects volatile main memory) with media failure (which affects non-volatile storage like disks), leading to incorrect recovery procedures.
Believing that a higher degree of isolation (e.g., Serializable) is always better, ignoring the significant performance trade-offs in concurrency.
